name: Rune
description: Single source of truth for the Rune language. The tree-sitter grammar, the syntax highlighter, the studio's registry copy (studio/data/keywords.json), the codegen templates, and the lint rule set (spec + generated, consumed by shape-checker) are all derived from this file. Hand-edit here (or via the studio) and regenerate with new/generate.mjs.

tags.label: Requirement
tags.description: Feature entry point / happy path. Format: noun.verb(InputDto): OutputDto
tags.summary: A requirement — one feature entry point, the happy-path end-to-end. Faults live on its steps, not here.
rules: Input must be a DTO (or inline {})
The last step must return the output DTO

tags.label: Module
tags.summary: Names the module this file describes; fills the <module>/ path slot. Optional (defaults to the filename).

tags.label: Entrypoint
tags.summary: An inbound entrypoint (HTTP route, CLI command, queue handler) — the inverse of a boundary. Its body references the [REQ] it dispatches to.

tags.label: Polymorphic step
tags.description: Opens a polymorphic block. The noun names an interface; each [CSE] is a concrete case.
tags.summary: Opens a polymorphic block where the noun names an interface. Each [CSE] is a concrete implementation; they all share the interface's return type. The block ends when indentation returns to step level.

tags.label: Case
tags.description: A concrete case inside a [PLY] block. Followed by a camelCase case name.
tags.summary: A concrete case inside a [PLY] block (8-space indent, camelCase name), with its own sub-steps and faults.

tags.label: Constructor
tags.description: Constructor shorthand. Instantiates a class and adds it to scope. No parens, no return type.
tags.summary: Constructor shorthand — instantiates a class and adds it to scope. No parentheses, no return type; construction details are an implementation concern.

tags.label: Return
tags.description: Return a value already created earlier in the flow. The value must be in scope.
tags.summary: Returns a value created earlier in the flow — useful when the last operation is a side effect (like saving to a DB) but you need to return a DTO made earlier. The value must be in scope.

tags.label: Type definition
tags.description: Defines a named semantic type that resolves to a primitive. Format: name: primitive
tags.summary: A named semantic type built on a primitive (string, number, boolean, void, Uint8Array, Class). Gives meaning to a raw primitive — `id` vs `string`. Generics (Array<url>), tuples ([x, y]), and string enums ("a" | "b") are allowed.

tags.label: DTO definition
tags.description: Defines a reusable data contract. Name must end in Dto. Format: NameDto: prop1, prop2
tags.summary: A reusable data contract; the name must end in Dto. Properties reference types or other DTOs. `url(s)` becomes `urls: Array<url>`. A description is required on the next line.
rules: All defined DTOs must be used

tags.label: Noun declaration
tags.description: Declares a noun (subject) with a prose description on the following lines.
tags.summary: Declares a noun (a subject / class) with a prose description on the following lines. Its purity (pure vs data) is inferred from whether it's used at a boundary.
rules: Grammar form; the spec is moving toward [TYP] noun: Class

boundaries.description: 2-char colon prefixes marking a step that crosses a system boundary.

dtoSuffix.description: An identifier ending in Dto is a DTO reference.

fault.description: Fault lines: lowercase, hyphen-separated, indented under a step.

lint.message: line exceeds {max} characters
{tag} should be indented {expected} spaces (found {found})
DTO name "{name}" must end in {suffix}
[DTO] {name} needs a description on the next line
requirement input "{input}" must be a DTO or inline {}
requirement output "{output}" must be a DTO
{prefix}: boundary params/return must be DTO or primitive — got {bad}
{key} has an inconsistent signature ({got} vs {first})
[TYP] {name} is defined more than once
[DTO] {name} is defined more than once
[TYP] {name} is never used
[DTO] {name} is never used
[NON] {name} is defined more than once
[NON] {name} is never used
step "{step}" is missing a return type
last step must return {output} (the REQ output), got {got}
[TYP] {name} must be a primitive — it can't reference DTO {ref}
instance noun "{noun}" isn't in scope (use :: for static, or return it from an earlier step)
parameter "{param}" isn't in scope (return it from a prior step or take it from the input DTO)
"{ref}" is not a defined [TYP], [DTO], [NON], or primitive
generated DTO is missing runtime validation (class-validator / zod)
fault "{fault}" from {req} has no Deno.test in the generated tests
generated file uses a "../" relative import — use an @ alias
generated file imports npm:/jsr: directly — use a # alias
integration file doesn't reference its DTO {dto}
{from} layer may not import {to} layer ({spec})
re-exports are only allowed in mod-root / poly-mod / bootstrap files
import into a poly structure must go through poly-mod ({spec})
module {from} may not import module {to}'s internals ({spec}) — use core/ or its mod-root
data-layer method returns a raw object — return a validated instance (assert/plainToInstance)
{count} sibling features export {name} with compatible signatures — extract a poly structure
feature extends poly base {base} — it likely belongs inside that poly structure
directory "{name}" is not allowed — name folders by role, not as generic buckets
loose grab-bag file "{name}" — put code in a named feature/role folder
module {module} has only {count} source files (< {min}) — consider consolidating
file sits in a rune-managed slot but no rune element declares it (orphan)

modifiers.label: Core
modifiers.description: Routes an element to the shared kernel (src/core/…) instead of the current module. Core elements are importable from any module; module elements are not. Allowed on [DTO] and [TYP]; not on [REQ].
